\boxed{B}

In this game, Black successfully navigated the **King’s Indian Defense** with precise pawn breaks and active piece play, while White’s aggressive pawn advances (e.g., e4, a4, a5) led to isolated pawns and weakened structure. Black’s counterplay in the center (e.g., ...d6-e5, ...c5) and piece activity (e.g., ...d4, ...c5) outplayed White’s over-extended pawns and exposed king. The final position favors Black due to White’s compromised pawn structure and lack of counterplay.
